Join a global network of researchers, developers, and enterprise innovators advancing the application of neutral-atom quantum computing.
The Pasqal Community is the hub where researchers, developers, and quantum enthusiasts come together to explore the exciting world of neutral-atom quantum computing.
The [Pasqal Community](https://community.pasqal.com/) brings together innovators, researchers, and enthusiasts from around the globe.

By joining, you'll gain access to learning resources, open source Pasqal tools, exclusive events, a dedicated Slack workspace, and technical support — all designed to help you connect, learn, and innovate.
Join topic-specific channels, participate in Q&A sessions with Pasqal's team, and attend virtual and in-person meetups where community members share knowledge and build connections.
Join topic-specific channels, participate in Q&A sessions with Pasqal's team, and attend virtual and in-person meetups where community members share knowledge and build connections.
Researchers targeting collaboration with peers to access Pasqal's neutral-atom environment and advance computational physics and hybrid algorithms.
Students who aspire to master quantum computing from its fundamentals to real-world applications and gain hands-on experience with Pasqal tools
Work on joint projects, provide operational feedback on developer tools, and participate in innovation challenges utilizing neutral-atom architecture.

interaction_phrase: IBM and Pasqal aim to develop
occurrence_sentence: IBM and Pasqal aim to develop a common approach to quantum-centric supercomputing and advance application-driven research, working with leading HPC institutions.
From Inception IBM and Pasqal are partnering to develop a unified approach to quantum-centric supercomputing, combining their expertise in superconducting and neutral atom quantum technologies.

interaction_phrase: IBM and Pasqal aim to develop
occurrence_sentence: IBM and Pasqal aim to develop a common approach to quantum-centric supercomputing and advance application-driven research, working with leading HPC institutions.
From Inception IBM and Pasqal are partnering to develop a unified approach to quantum-centric supercomputing, combining their expertise in superconducting and neutral atom quantum technologies.
IBM and Pasqal are collaborating on open-source infrastructure targeting a unified, hardware-agnostic programming model for quantum-centric supercomputing.
IBM and Pasqal are collaborating on open-source infrastructure targeting a unified, hardware-agnostic programming model for quantum-centric supercomputing.

The Open Quantum Institute (OQI) is a multilateral governance initiative that promotes global and inclusive access to quantum computing and the development of applications for the benefit of humanity. As a novel science diplomacy instrument, it brings together academia, diplomacy, private sector and philanthropy stakeholders. OQI is hosted by CERN during its pilot phase (2024-2026).
OQI is hosted by CERN during its pilot phase (2024-2026).

interaction_phrase: serves as a crucial provider of solutions and services for universities
occurrence_sentence: Cineca stands as one of Italy's largest computing centers and is globally recognized for its leadership in High Performance Computing (HPC). Simultaneously, Cineca serves as a crucial provider of solutions and services for universities, research centers, the Ministry of Education, the Ministry of University and Research, and other institutions.

The Université de Sherbrooke is in the heart of one of Quebec's three major research hubs. Known for its sense of innovation, the Université de Sherbrooke is a key partner of senior and regional governments in the promotion of economic, cultural, and social development.

Pasqal developed together with IBM Quantum a comprehensive [Quantum Advantage Framework](https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.20658) that defines what trustworthy quantum advantage truly means—setting clear, measurable criteria to verify results and quantify performance beyond classical computing.

Pasqal developed together with IBM Quantum a comprehensive Quantum Advantage Framework that defines what trustworthy quantum advantage truly means—setting clear, measurable criteria to verify results and quantify performance beyond classical computing.
our latest work shows materials simulation as the clearest path to near-term quantum advantage under the IBM framework.

Pasqal's neutral-atom processors act as quantum microscopes, mimicking these systems—our latest work shows materials simulation as the clearest path to near-term quantum advantage under the IBM framework.
Auxiliary spin Hybrid quantum-classical analog simulation of two-dimensional Fermi-Hubbard models with neutral atoms [arXiv 2510.05897](https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.05897)
NUMERICS BENCHMARKS Simulating dynamics of the two-dimensional transverse-field Ising model: a comparative study of large-scale classical numerics [arXiv 2511.20388](https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.20388)
energy consumption Resource assessment of classical and quantum hardware for post-quench dynamics [arXiv 2511.19340](https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.19340)
